metadata.scheme_name: Incentive Scheme for Meritorious Students of Economically Backward Class of General Category

raw_data.details: The "Incentive Scheme for Meritorious Students of Economically Backward Class of General Category" was launched by the Education Department, Government of Rajasthan. Under this scheme, a one-time financial assistance of ₹15,000 along with a certificate of appreciation is awarded to the top 100 meritorious students of Class 10 and each stream (Science, Commerce, Arts) of Class 12, who are listed in the state-level merit list of the Rajasthan Board of Secondary Education (RBSE), Ajmer. The scheme is implemented by the Directorate of Secondary Education, Rajasthan, Bikaner.
raw_data.benefits: Financial Assistance:One-time financial assistance of ₹15,000 is provided to economically backward students from the general category studying in government and private schools. Certificate of Appreciation:An official certificate is awarded along with the monetary benefit.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan. The applicant should be a student. The applicant should belong to the economically backward class from the general category. The applicant should be studying in a government or private school. The applicant should have passed Class 10th, Praveshika, Class 12th (Science/Commerce/Arts), or Varishta Upadhyay examination conducted by Rajasthan Board of Secondary Education (RBSE), Ajmer. The applicant should have secured a minimum of 75% marks in the qualifying examination. The applicant’s parents’ combined annual income from all sources should be less than ₹2,50,000.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineApplication Process:
Step 1: To avail of the benefits of the scheme, students should fill out the online application form through the institution head (principal) of their respective school.
Step 2: Eligible students are identified by the head of their institution.
Step 3: Submit copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required) to the respective school.
Step 4: The school will visit the [Shala Darpan, Rajasthan](https://rajshaladarpan.rajasthan.gov.in/SD4/Home/Public2/Default.aspx) Portal.
Step 5: Using the school's login credentials (Login ID and Password), the school will fill and submit the application form. Post-Application Process:
Step 1: The institution head forwards the online application to the concerned District Education Officer (DEO) Headquarters (Primary/Secondary Education).
Step 2: The District Education Officer compiles and forwards the verified applications of all eligible students from the district to the Directorate of Secondary Education, Rajasthan, Bikaner.
Step 3: Post-verification, the incentive amount is disbursed to the students' bank accounts.
raw_data.documents_required: Aadhaar Card or Jan Aadhaar Card of the StudentMarksheet CopyIncome Certificate /EWS certificateAny other documents as required

To know more please visit Check EligibilityRajasthanIncentive Scheme for Meritorious Students of Economically Backward Class of General CategoryEconomically Backward ClassFinancial AssistanceMeritoriousStudentDetailsThe "Incentive Scheme for Meritorious Students of Economically Backward Class of General Category" was launched by the Education Department, Government of Rajasthan. Under this scheme, a one-time financial assistance of ₹15,000 along with a certificate of appreciation is awarded to the top 100 meritorious students of Class 10 and each stream (Science, Commerce, Arts) of Class 12, who are listed in the state-level merit list of the Rajasthan Board of Secondary Education (RBSE), Ajmer. The scheme is implemented by the Directorate of Secondary Education, Rajasthan, Bikaner. BenefitsFinancial Assistance:One-time financial assistance of ₹15,000 is provided to economically backward students from the general category studying in government and private schools. Certificate of Appreciation:An official certificate is awarded along with the monetary benefit. EligibilityThe applicant should be a native of Rajasthan. The applicant should be a student. The applicant should belong to the economically backward class from the general category. The applicant should be studying in a government or private school. The applicant should have passed Class 10th, Praveshika, Class 12th (Science/Commerce/Arts), or Varishta Upadhyay examination conducted by Rajasthan Board of Secondary Education (RBSE), Ajmer. The applicant should have secured a minimum of 75% marks in the qualifying examination. The applicant’s parents’ combined annual income from all sources should be less than ₹2,50,000. Application ProcessOnlineApplication Process:Step 1: To avail of the benefits of the scheme, students should fill out the online application form through the institution head (principal) of their respective school. Step 2: Eligible students are identified by the head of their institution. Step 3: Submit copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required) to the respective school. Step 4: The school will visit the [Shala Darpan, Rajasthan](https://rajshaladarpan.rajasthan.gov.in/SD4/Home/Public2/Default.aspx) Portal. Step 5: Using the school's login credentials (Login ID and Password), the school will fill and submit the application form. Post-Application Process:Step 1: The institution head forwards the online application to the concerned District Education Officer (DEO) Headquarters (Primary/Secondary Education). Step 2: The District Education Officer compiles and forwards the verified applications of all eligible students from the district to the Directorate of Secondary Education, Rajasthan, Bikaner. Step 3: Post-verification, the incentive amount is disbursed to the students' bank accounts. Documents RequiredAadhaar Card or Jan Aadhaar Card of the StudentMarksheet CopyIncome Certificate /EWS certificateAny other documents as requiredFrequently Asked QuestionsWho implements the scheme? The Directorate of Secondary Education, Rajasthan, Bikaner. How much financial benefit is provided under the scheme? A one-time financial assistance of ₹15,000. What additional benefit is provided apart from the cash incentive? A certificate of appreciation. Who is eligible for this scheme? General category students with 75% or above marks and parental income below ₹2,50,000 per annum. Which classes' students are eligible for the scheme? Students of Class 10th, Praveshika, Class 12th (Science, Commerce, Arts), and Varishta Upadhyay. How many students benefit each year? A total of 400 students annually. What is the minimum percentage required for eligibility? At least 75% marks in the qualifying examination. What documents are required to apply? Aadhaar/Jan Aadhaar Card, Marksheet, and Income Certificate. How can a student apply for this scheme? Through their school Principal via online application. Who verifies and forwards the applications? The concerned District Education Officer (Primary/Secondary Education). When is the amount disbursed? After verification of merit and eligibility. Is this benefit provided every year? The benefit is given only once for each qualifying examination. raw_data.scheme_name: Incentive Scheme for Meritorious Students of Economically Backward Class of General Category

faqs.question: Who implements the scheme?
faqs.answer: The Directorate of Secondary Education, Rajasthan, Bikaner.

faqs.question: How much financial benefit is provided under the scheme?
What additional benefit is provided apart from the cash incentive?
Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: General category students with 75% or above marks and parental income below ₹2,50,000 per annum.

faqs.question: Which classes' students are eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Students of Class 10th, Praveshika, Class 12th (Science, Commerce, Arts), and Varishta Upadhyay.

faqs.question: How many students benefit each year?
What is the minimum percentage required for eligibility?
faqs.answer: At least 75% marks in the qualifying examination.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply?
faqs.answer: Aadhaar/Jan Aadhaar Card, Marksheet, and Income Certificate.

faqs.question: How can a student apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Through their school Principal via online application.

faqs.question: Who verifies and forwards the applications?
faqs.answer: The concerned District Education Officer (Primary/Secondary Education).

faqs.question: When is the amount disbursed?
faqs.answer: After verification of merit and eligibility.

faqs.question: Is this benefit provided every year?
faqs.answer: The benefit is given only once for each qualifying examination.
